title: hieracium murorum subsp. pictorum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. pictorum, also known as wall hawk's-beard or wall hawkweed, is a perennial herb native to Europe, belonging to the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as nausea, diarrhea, and indigestion, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort. The plant's health benefits also include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. Hieracium murorum subsp. pictorum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
